---
title: "Phase 3: Evaluation Harness"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Ollama required for live scores.**
This page shows the evaluation framework and test set. Running RAGAS to
produce actual scores requires Ollama running with `llama3` and the ECB
speeches ingested into ChromaDB. See
[RAG Testing Methodology](rag_testing_methodology.qmd) for the full approach.
:::

Related issues:
[#9 Gold-standard test set](https://github.com/almac09/private-rag-assistant/issues/9) (closed),
[#10 RAGAS scoring](https://github.com/almac09/private-rag-assistant/issues/10) (closed),
[#11 Wrap evaluation in pytest](https://github.com/almac09/private-rag-assistant/issues/11) (closed),
[#12 Publish results](https://github.com/almac09/private-rag-assistant/issues/12) (closed).

---

## Test question set

`tests/test_questions.json` contains 20 fixed questions drawn from ECB monetary
policy topics. The set is versioned and does not change between evaluation runs.

```{python}
import sys, json
sys.path.insert(0, "..")

from pathlib import Path
from IPython.display import HTML, display

with open("../tests/test_questions.json") as f:
    questions = json.load(f)

print(f"Total questions : {len(questions)}")
print(f"In corpus       : {sum(1 for q in questions if q.get('in_corpus', True))}")
print(f"Abstention      : {sum(1 for q in questions if not q.get('in_corpus', True))}")

all_tags = sorted({t for q in questions for t in q.get('tags', [])})
print(f"Tag types       : {all_tags}")
```

### Question breakdown by tag

```{python}
from collections import Counter

tag_counts = Counter(t for q in questions for t in q.get("tags", []))

rows = "".join(
    f"<tr><td style='padding:4px 8px'>{tag}</td>"
    f"<td style='padding:4px 8px'>{count}</td></tr>"
    for tag, count in sorted(tag_counts.items())
)
display(HTML(
    "<table style='border-collapse:collapse'>"
    "<thead><tr style='border-bottom:2px solid #dee2e6'>"
    "<th style='padding:4px 8px'>Tag</th><th style='padding:4px 8px'>Count</th>"
    "</tr></thead><tbody>" + rows + "</tbody></table>"
))
```

### Sample questions

```{python}
sample_ids = ["q001", "q005", "q010", "q018"]
sample = [q for q in questions if q["id"] in sample_ids]

rows = "".join(
    f"<tr>"
    f"<td style='padding:6px 8px;font-family:monospace'>{q['id']}</td>"
    f"<td style='padding:6px 8px'>{q['question']}</td>"
    f"<td style='padding:6px 8px;text-align:center'>{'✓' if q.get('in_corpus') else '✗ abstention'}</td>"
    f"<td style='padding:6px 8px'>{', '.join(q.get('tags', []))}</td>"
    f"</tr>"
    for q in sample
)
display(HTML(
    "<table style='width:100%;border-collapse:collapse'>"
    "<thead><tr style='border-bottom:2px solid #dee2e6'>"
    "<th style='padding:6px 8px'>ID</th><th style='padding:6px 8px'>Question</th>"
    "<th style='padding:6px 8px'>In corpus</th><th style='padding:6px 8px'>Tags</th>"
    "</tr></thead><tbody>" + rows + "</tbody></table>"
))
```

---

## Evaluation pipeline

`rag.evaluate` provides three functions that compose into the full evaluation loop:

```{python}
display(HTML(
    "<table style='width:100%;border-collapse:collapse'>"
    "<thead><tr style='border-bottom:2px solid #dee2e6'>"
    "<th style='padding:6px;text-align:left'>Function</th>"
    "<th style='padding:6px;text-align:left'>Input → Output</th>"
    "</tr></thead><tbody>"
    "<tr><td style='padding:6px'><code>load_test_questions()</code></td>"
    "<td style='padding:6px'>JSON path → list of question dicts (validates required keys)</td></tr>"
    "<tr><td style='padding:6px'><code>run_evaluation()</code></td>"
    "<td style='padding:6px'>Chain + questions → RAGAS scores + per-question results dict</td></tr>"
    "<tr><td style='padding:6px'><code>save_results()</code></td>"
    "<td style='padding:6px'>Results dict → <code>tests/results/phase_N_YYYYMMDD.json</code></td></tr>"
    "</tbody></table>"
))
```

### Running the evaluation locally

```python
# Requires: ollama serve + ChromaDB populated (run scripts/ollama_ready.py first)
from rag.query import load_vectorstore, build_rag_chain
from rag.evaluate import load_test_questions, run_evaluation, save_results

vectorstore = load_vectorstore()
chain = build_rag_chain(vectorstore, model="llama3", k=4)
questions = load_test_questions("tests/test_questions.json")

results = run_evaluation(chain, questions, phase="phase_2")
out = save_results(results)
print(f"Results saved to {out}")
print(results["scores"])
```

---

## RAGAS metrics

The four metrics scored for every question:

```{python}
metrics = [
    ("Faithfulness", "Does the answer contradict the retrieved chunks?",
     "Low → model is hallucinating or ignoring context"),
    ("Answer Relevancy", "Does the answer address the question?",
     "Low → answer is off-topic or evasive"),
    ("Context Precision", "Are retrieved chunks relevant to the question?",
     "Low → retriever is pulling back noise"),
    ("Context Recall", "Does the retrieved context contain what's needed?",
     "Low → relevant material isn't being found"),
]

rows = "".join(
    f"<tr><td style='padding:6px'><strong>{m}</strong></td>"
    f"<td style='padding:6px'>{what}</td>"
    f"<td style='padding:6px'>{interp}</td></tr>"
    for m, what, interp in metrics
)
display(HTML(
    "<table style='width:100%;border-collapse:collapse'>"
    "<thead><tr style='border-bottom:2px solid #dee2e6'>"
    "<th style='padding:6px'>Metric</th><th style='padding:6px'>Measures</th>"
    "<th style='padding:6px'>Low score means</th>"
    "</tr></thead><tbody>" + rows + "</tbody></table>"
))
```

---

## Results

Results files are saved to `tests/results/` after each evaluation run and named
`phase_<label>_<YYYYMMDD>.json`. The table below is populated from the most
recent file if one exists.

```{python}
import os
results_dir = Path("../tests/results")

if results_dir.exists() and any(results_dir.glob("*.json")):
    latest = sorted(results_dir.glob("*.json"))[-1]
    with open(latest) as f:
        r = json.load(f)

    print(f"Phase     : {r.get('phase')}")
    print(f"Timestamp : {r.get('timestamp')}")
    print(f"Questions : {r.get('n_questions')}")
    print()
    scores = r.get("scores", {})
    for metric, value in scores.items():
        bar = "█" * int(value * 20)
        print(f"  {metric:<22} {value:.3f}  {bar}")
else:
    print("No results files found in tests/results/.")
    print("Run the evaluation locally with Ollama to generate scores.")
```

See [RAG Testing Methodology](rag_testing_methodology.qmd) for interpretation
guidance and known limitations of each metric.
